questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Consider the two reactions i.e. halogenation and isotopic substitution given. When kinetics studies were carried out for both reactions, it was found that their rates were similar when identical reaction conditions i.e. acid/base were used. Based on this statement what can you infer about the reaction?
options: Both reactions have a common intermediate and its formation is the rate determining step
Both reactions have a common intermediate and its formation occurs after the rate determining step
The halogenation will be unaffected by concentration of bromine
questions.solution: formation of enol or enolate is the RDS. Hence the rate of reaction is unaffected by the nature or concentration of halogen
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Haloform reaction with $\mathrm{I}_{2}$ and $\mathrm{KOH}$ will be responded by
questions.solution:
Haloform reaction with $I_{2}$ and $K O H$
Thus, options $(a, b)$ are correct.

questions.q: The correct statements about (I) and (II) are :
options: The acid catalysed hydrolysis of (I) and (II) yields one product identical.
For Wolff Kishner reduction (I) is the better reactant.
Reduction by $\mathrm{LiAlH}_4$ forms a diol in both the cases.
questions.solution: Wolff Kishner reduction is not used in presence of base sensitive group.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
